name: Zoning
description: From 1999 thru March 30, 2015, records show a total of 28 ZAA (Zoning Amendments, e.g. County-approved zoning changes) decisions were issued.  It is not known what, if any, processes or procedures existed to update the Zoning layer when a ZAA was approved.  It is likely that some, but not all, of these changes are reflected in the Zoning Layer.  It is possible that none of these changes are correctly reflected in the Zoning layer. Annexations since 1999 may not be accurately reflected in this la

name: WaterSystems
description: This layer shows water system areas and boundaries in Island County as of 2007.  The source for each feature is listed in the attribute table.  Updates to this layer started in 2017 using Planning and Health Dept data.  Various issues of data quality have arose with the delay in updates to this layer.  This should be used strictly as a reference.

name: Watersheds
description: Island County Watersheds used for the Surface Water Quality Monitoring Program.  This layer does not include coastal drainages and smaller watersheds. This layer was created in the Planning Department using 2001 LiDAR between 2005 and 2008.  Ground truthing edits were made through 2012.

name: Voting Precincts
description: The Voting Precincts layer is intended for use by Auditors Office and others for the purposes of displaying voting precincts and identifying precinct boundaries.

name: UGA Boundaries
description: The layer shows the current City Limits, Urban Growth Area (UGA) and Joint Planning Area (JPA) classifications for all of Island County, pursuit to ICC 17.03.  This layer has been maintained with updates to the UGA during the comprehensive plan update.  Layer is accurate to the parcel level.

name: Trails
description: Trails data is used by the public for recreational purposes and by agencies for management and planning purposes.  Trails data was collected by GPS, tracing on aerial photos and lidar hillshades, and by rough estimates.  Some of the data was imported from the state of washington at http://services.arcgis.com/jsIt88o09Q0r1j8h/ArcGIS/rest/services/WATrails_State/FeatureServer.

name: Tax Code Area
description: This layer is used frequently by many departments to outline junior taxing districts. Some attributes are utilized more frequently than others. The layer is used for map reference, cartography, and answering public requests. This feature class is derived from the parcel layer; therefore it is accurate to the level of the parcels.

name: Shoreline Designations
description: This is the Shoreline Jurisdiction designation shapefile for the Island County SMP Update of 2012.The Shoreline Master Program this map shapefile is associated with was locally adopted in Island County under Resolution C-125-12, PLG-007-12 on 12/27/2012.As of 1/8/2015, this locally adopted SMP was still under review by Dept of Ecology, and had not been formally adopted by Island County. As such, this map is still technically 'proposed' and doe snot have regulatory authority as of this date of 1/

name: Roads
description: This feature class was created for the following uses: a.) Geocoding using street centerline distance interpolation, b.) identification of ownership and jurisdiction of roads within the county, c.) maintenance, mapping, and analysis of speed zones, d.) maintenance, mapping, and analysis of federal functional classes (FCC), and e.) general cartographic uses.  The road lines are generally within a few feet of the road centerlines as seen on aerial photos.

name: RoadLog Mobility
description: This layer is a comprehensive depiction of roads within Island County that have an assigned MOBILITY Road Log Number. Road log numbers are used by the County for storing and managing most roads engineering data. Most importantly, the road log numbers correlate to the CRAB's MOBILITY database. By default Roads_Mobility consists primarily of roads owned by Island County. The centerlines of this layer were copied from Roads_Address. Roads_Address was photogrammetrically generated from 2007 aerial p

name: Plats
description: Plat and condo boundaries for subdivisions and condominiums within Island County.  Accuracy at the parcel level as boundaries are crated by merging parcels within the plat to create a boundary.

name: Parks
description: Park boundaries.  Merged parcels of related ownership.  Boundaries are for county owned parks as well as other entities.  Attributes show park amenities, contact info, links to park maps. Data layer creation in 2016 and has remained updated since.

name: Parcels
description: Parcel boundaries exported nightly from the Assessor's Office managed parcel fabric and joined with attributes related to owner information, values and size, the water source (Public Health database) and a link to the SmartGov public portal (permitting database).  Most features are within 3 feet however some features can be up to 20 feet off.  Please read the full data disclaimer when using this dataset.

name: Diking Drainage Districts
description: This layer is used frequently by many departments to outline junior taxing districts. Some attributes are utilized more frequently than others. The layer is used for map reference, cartography, and answering public requests. This feature class is derived from the parcel layer; therefore it is accurate to the level of the parcels.

name: Culverts
description: The culvert data were originally collected in the mid-2000s by road shop crews using a Trimble GPS collector. Data are updated as new culverts are installed or existing but unmapped culverts are discovered. Descriptive information about each culvert, including size, material, condition, and association with tide gates is included in the attributes.The “House_Numb” field is used if the feature is near a known street address. “Location” is used when descriptive information about the feature’s loca

name: City Limits
description: Boundaries of the City Limits within Island County.  Data created from the Tax Code Area and Parcel Layers.  Accurate to the Parcel level.

name: Catch Basins
description: The catch basins data were originally collected in the mid-2000s by road shop crews using a Trimble GPS collector. Data are updated as new catch basins are installed or existing but unmapped catch basins are discovered. Accuracy of GPS ranges from a couple feet to around 15'+. Descriptive information about each catch basin, including type, depth, size and condition, is included in the attributes. The “House_Numb” field is used if the feature is near a known street address. “Location” is used whe

name: Average Daily Trips
description: County roads with Average Daily Trip (ADT) values.  ADT values are from a variety of sources, including actual counts and estimates.  ADT data is stored on the Mobility road management software and data system established by the Washington County Road Administration Board (http://www.crab.wa.gov/Technology/Mobility/).

name: Aircraft Accident Potential
description: This layer was provided by the U.S. Navy (NAS Whidbey) in about 2007.  This layer shows the location of the Clear Zones at NAS Whidbey and the OLF, and the Accident Potential Zone surrounding NAS Whidbey.  This layer is used by ICP&CD for land use regulation near the naval airfields on the island. Island County does not have jurisdiction over the APZ location, size or shape, as this is set by the Navy.  Island County chose to adopt into its local Zoning Code the APZ as delineated by the Navy.  I

name: Aircraft Accident Potential
description: This layer was provided by the U.S. Navy (NAS Whidbey) in about 2007.  This layer shows the location of the Clear Zones at NAS Whidbey and the OLF, and the Accident Potential Zone surrounding NAS Whidbey.  This layer is used by ICP&CD for land use regulation near the naval airfields on the island. Island County does not have jurisdiction over the APZ location, size or shape, as this is set by the Navy.  Island County chose to adopt into its local Zoning Code the APZ as delineated by the Navy.  I

name: Wetlands
description: This dataset was composed in 2006 with the initiation of the Island County Critical Area Update.  During the process wetland information was compiled from three main resources:  The National Wetland Inventory Dataset, digital wetland delineations performed by Pentec in 1998, and county wetland files.  Approximately 750, 600, and 400 shapes respectively were merged together to form this single dataset.  Many of the same wetlands were found in each dataset thus when combined the total number to we

name: Burn Permits
description: Issued burn permits from the SmartGov permitting database joined to the Island County parcel layer.  Permits shown in this layer are burn permits that are active.  This layer also contains a link to the online permitting system portal for each permit.

name: Streams Cartographic
description: This data layer was created for the purposes of providing a more spatially accurate watercourses layer than those available from WDNR.  It is intended for cartographic purposes.  It is not intended for regulatory purposes and contains no regulatory attribute data.  The data has not been field verified.

name: Lakes and Ponds
description: Data was created by tracing water features using 2007 and 2014 aerial photographs and 2001 and 2014 lidar data. Data were collected using methods that are accurate to within 10 meters/30 feet

name: Quarter Sections
description: Quarter Section boundaries created from past quarter section layer, parcel layer and mylar map boundaries while georeferencing in Island County Assessor's Office.  Accuracy undetermined.

name: Steep Slopes Raster Data
description: Data were derived from 2014 6" resolution Island County lidar data using ArcGIS and Spatial Analyst Tools. The resulting raster was then converted to polygons.  Polygons spanning elevation differences <10' were deleted.

name: Ballot Boxes
description: The ballot box layer was created for populating ballot boxes on an interactive public facing web application for elections department.  Point was placed as close as possible to the location of the box, however accuracy is at the address level; within 100.’

name: Unstable Slopes
description: These maps are intended to educate the public about Washington's shoreline and to guide regional land use decisions. These maps should not be used as a substitute for site-specific studies carried out by qualified geologists and engineers. The Department of Ecology assumes no liability for the data depicted on these maps.

name: Noise Contours
description: This GIS dataset was created to identify the day-night average noise levels around both Ault Field and Outlying Field (OLF) Coupeville. This created the Air Installations Compatible Use Zones (AICUZ) noise contours. This was revised in 2005 through a study done by the Navy in March 2005. The Onyx Group was the primary consultants for this study. The GIS work for the AICUZ noise zones was done by Wyle Laboratories in California.

name: Steep Slopes
description: Data were derived from 2014 Island County lidar data using Spatial Analyst Surface Tools, then converted to polygon feature class.  Polygons spanning elevation difference <10' were deleted.

name: 5' Contour Shapefile
description: 5' Contours created from 2015 6" resolution Lidar data at a county-wide scale.  Intended uses are for cartographic and analysis purposes.

name: 25' Contour Shapefile
description: 25' contours created from 2015 6" resolution Lidar data at a county-wide scale. Intended uses are for cartographic and analysis purposes.

name: Road Conditions
description: Island County Road Conditions- Showing where (semi) or permanent road closures and one-way direction roads are located.  Road Closures and one-ways are permanent until it is determined the road should be re-built.

name: Road Closures
description: Island County Road Conditions- Showing where (semi) or permanent road closures and one-way direction roads are located.  Road Closures and one-ways are permanent until it is determined the road should be re-built.

name: One Way Directions
description: Island County Road Conditions- Showing where (semi) or permanent road closures and one-way direction roads are located.  Road Closures and one-ways are permanent until it is determined the road should be re-built.

name: Tideland only Parcels
description: Points of Tidal properties, intended for use by the Assessor’s Office. This layer only reflects tide parcels that have a different parcel number than the adjacent land parcel. A point represents the relative location of the parcel rather than a polygon to demonstrate that the parcel exists, however the boundaries are not drawn on a map due to the difficulty in tracking the dimensions of the parcel as the borders change over time.  Use of the layer assists the appraisers and public in locating th

name: Access Utility Permits
description: This data layer is used in two web maps, one accessible to the public and on the website as well as one that is only accessible to Public Works staff.  The web map is to assist in knowing where access/utility permits are located when working out in the field.
